TYPE

Elixir Cards — all 1⚡, pick 10

Rules & Gameplay

Complete Edition · 2–4 Players · Physical Card Game

⚡ Elixir System

Each deck includes exactly 10 Elixir Cards. Keep them in a separate face-down pile. At the start of each turn, flip the top 2 Elixir Cards face-up — each face-up card = 1⚡. To spend elixir, rotate the card 90° (landscape). At the start of your next turn, rotate all spent cards back up and flip a new one if available.

Round startAll refresh + 1 new flipped = 3⚡
  • Game start: Flip the top 2 Elixir Cards face-up (start with 2⚡)
  • Each turn: Rotate spent cards back up-right, then flip 1 new card
  • Spending: Rotate a face-up Elixir Card 90° sideways = spend 1⚡
  • Maximum: You can accumulate up to 10⚡ (all 10 Elixir Cards face-up)

⚔ Turn Order

  1. Deploy Cards: Spend elixir to play unit cards to your Back row, or deploy directly to the Neutral Battle Zone if you prefer an aggressive push.
  2. Advance Units: All non-Building units automatically move 1 row forward (Back → Front → Neutral Battle Zone). Buildings never move.
  3. Combat: Roll D20 for every unit on the board. Units in the Neutral Zone fight first, then Front row, then Back row.
  4. Tower Counterattack: Each standing Princess Tower fires 1 flat wound at the nearest enemy unit in either lane. King Tower fires when active.
  5. Opponent's Turn: Other player(s) take their turn in the same order.

🗺️ The Board — 5 Zones

🔴 ENEMY BACK ROW — units spawn here
🔴 ENEMY FRONT ROW — advancing to battle
⚔ NEUTRAL BATTLE ZONE — both sides fight here
🟣 YOUR FRONT ROW — advancing to battle
🟣 YOUR BACK ROW — deploy here

Each player has Lane A and Lane B running side-by-side. Units in the same lane fight each other.

🎯 Attack Distance

Not every unit can attack from every position. Attack range determines which zone a unit can reach:

⚔ GROUND / AIR
1-space range
  • Back row: Cannot attack — too far from battle
  • Front row: Can attack enemies in the Neutral Zone only
  • Neutral Zone: Attacks all enemy units + Princess Tower if lane is clear
🏹 RANGED
2-space range
  • Back row: Can fire into the Neutral Zone
  • Front row: Can attack Neutral + hit Princess Tower directly
  • Neutral Zone: Attacks all enemy units + towers
BUILDING
1-zone forward
  • Any position: Fires at enemies 1 zone ahead (toward enemy tower)
  • Never attacks Princess or King Towers directly
  • Never moves — stays in the row it was deployed to

🎲 D20 Combat Table

Every attack rolls a D20. One D6 die sits beside each unit on the board, rotated up as wounds are taken.

D20 RollResultEffect
1MISSNo wounds dealt
2 – 8WEAK HIT1 wound
9 – 16HIT2 wounds
17 – 19CRIT3 wounds
20⚡ CLASH STRIKE3 wounds + trigger the card's special Clash Strike effect

When a unit reaches its Wounds to Kill limit, it's eliminated. If it has an ☠ ON DEATH effect (orange box on card), that triggers immediately.

🏰 Towers & Winning

  • Princess Towers (8 wounds): One per lane per player. They fire 1 wound per round at the nearest enemy unit in either lane. Falls at 8 wounds.
  • King Tower (12 wounds): Becomes active the moment either Princess Tower falls. Fires 1 wound per round covering both lanes.
  • Win condition: Destroy the enemy King Tower.
  • Tower targeting: Towers always pick the nearest enemy unit regardless of lane — they protect both lanes at once.

🃏 Card Types

⚔ Ground
Standard melee. Advances Back→Front→Neutral each turn. 1-space attack range. Must reach Neutral Zone to attack towers.
🐉 Air
Same movement as Ground. 1-space range. Ground units CAN attack Air units — they're not immune.
🏹 Ranged
2-space range. Can fire from Back row into Neutral Zone. Can hit Princess Tower from Front row without reaching Neutral.
Building
Never moves. Attacks 1 zone ahead each turn. Never attacks towers directly. Good for defending key rows.
⚡ Spell
Instant effect, no D20 roll. Costs elixir and is discarded after use. See card text for exact effect.

🐢 Movement Speed

⚡ Fast
Deploys directly to Front row (skips Back). Still takes 1 turn to reach the Neutral Zone.
🧍 Normal
Deploys to Back row. Takes 2 turns to reach the Neutral Zone.
🐢 Slow
Deploys to Back row. Takes 2 turns to reach the Neutral Zone. Often has higher wounds or special effects to compensate.
— Fixed
Building. Never advances. Placed where deployed permanently.

🃏 Card Effects

Cards have up to three types of special effects. Each is shown in its own colored box on the card.

🟢 ON DEPLOY
Green box on card. Triggers the moment the card is placed, before any combat that turn. Examples: Royal Recruits spawns 2 tokens immediately; PEKKA deals 1w to all enemies in the zone on entry; Mega Knight lands and deals 1w to everyone in the zone.
☠ ON DEATH
Orange box on card. Triggers the moment the unit is eliminated from any zone. Examples: spawn skeleton tokens, deal wounds to nearby enemies, heal allies. Cannot be prevented.
⚡ CLASH STRIKE
Purple box on card. Triggers on a D20 natural 20. Deals 3 wounds PLUS the card's unique special effect. Every unit has a unique Clash Strike — check the card for details.

Skeleton Tokens: Some ON DEPLOY / ON DEATH / CS effects spawn Skeleton tokens — use a spare D6 laid flat on the board. Each token is 2-wound, Fast, Ground. They fight like normal units.

📦 Deck Rules

  • 12 unit/spell cards — any combination from any set(s). Maximum 2 copies of any one card.
  • 10 Elixir Cards — mandatory, any combination of the 4 types (one per set). Mix and match freely.
  • Requirements: At least 1 Tank (5+ wound Ground), 1 Air unit, 1 Ranged unit, 1 Building, 1 Spell, 1 Card per set.
  • Holographic cards are identical in stats — purely cosmetic collector's versions.

🧰 Physical Components

  • Cards: Print the Card PDF on regular paper, cut to size, optionally sleeve
  • D6 dice: 1 per unit on board (track wounds by rotating face). 8× recommended.
  • D8: 1 for Princess Tower health (or use D20 + tally)
  • D12: 1 for King Tower health
  • D20: 1 for all combat rolls
  • Board: Print the 3-sheet landscape PDF and tape together. Or lay out on a felt surface.

🧠 Suggested Strategies

New to the game? These combos are a great place to start.
⚡ The Skeleton Swarm

Cards: Skeletons + Skeleton Army + Skeleton King + Rage

Deploy cheap Skeletons early to chip away at the enemy. Once the Skeleton King is on the board, every CS summons more skeletons — then drop Rage to surge the whole swarm forward at once. Your opponent will be overwhelmed trying to wound them all before they reach the tower.

💡 Tip: Use Skeleton Army as a surprise blocker when an enemy rushes your lane — 5 fast units for only 3⚡ is exceptional value.

🏰 Tank & Punish

Cards: Royal Giant + Rune Giant + Royal Delivery + Rocket

Send a Royal Giant or Rune Giant down a lane to soak up tower hits while your opponent panics. Meanwhile, Royal Delivery drops a free Knight token to distract defenders. Once the tower is low, finish it with Rocket for a massive 5-wound kill shot from range.

💡 Tip: The Rune Giant gains rune buffs on CS — try to trigger it before it hits the Front row so it arrives extra powerful.

💨 Fast Lane Blitz

Cards: Prince + Ram Rider + Royal Hogs + Rage

All four of these units have Fast speed — they move aggressively and reach the enemy tower before a slow opponent can react. Ram Rider's CS shoves enemies back a row, Prince charges for bonus advance on CS, and Royal Hogs push on CS too. Layer Rage on top and the whole lane surges at once.

💡 Tip: Focus everything on ONE lane rather than splitting — overwhelming a single lane is faster than grinding both.

🌿 Poison Control

Cards: Witch + Poison + Princess + Musketeer

Play defensively and grind the opponent down. Witch spawns skeletons as blockers on deploy, Princess hits through targets from the Back row, and Musketeer provides steady long-range damage. Drop Poison when the enemy lane is stacked — it deals +1w to every unit and the tower simultaneously, punishing anyone who over-commits.

💡 Tip: Princess + Musketeer in the same lane means two ranged units firing from safety — stack them both and your opponent has to fight through a wall of ranged fire just to reach your tower.

2v2 Expansion

4 Players · 2 Teams · One Lane Each · Shared Towers

Four players — two per team. Each player controls one lane and manages their own Elixir Deck. Teammates share towers but play independently. Communication and timing are everything.

👥 Team Setup

⚔️ TEAM ALPHA
P1
Player 1
Controls Lane A · Own Elixir Deck
P2
Player 2
Controls Lane B · Own Elixir Deck

Shared: 2× Princess Towers + 1× King Tower. Each player has their own 12-card unit deck and 10-card Elixir Deck.

🛡 TEAM OMEGA
P3
Player 3
Controls Lane A · Own Elixir Deck
P4
Player 4
Controls Lane B · Own Elixir Deck

P3 faces P1 across Lane A. P4 faces P2 across Lane B. Same tower layout as Team Alpha.

🔄 2v2 Turn Order

Teams alternate so both lanes are always active each half-round.

P1 Lane A
→
P3 Lane A
→
P2 Lane B
→
P4 Lane B
→
repeat

Each player completes their full 6-step turn before the next player goes. During your turn you may whisper briefly to your teammate. You may not show your hand or reveal exact elixir counts.

🤝 Assist Mechanics

⚡ ELIXIR PASS
1

On your turn, you may rotate one of your available Elixir Cards face-down and place it in front of your teammate. They flip it face-up on their next turn and gain 1⚡ from it. Limit: 1 pass per turn.

🗺️ CROSS-LANE PLAY
2

You may play a unit into your teammate's lane instead of your own — but it costs +1 extra elixir (coordination penalty). That unit then belongs to the lane's owner for the rest of the game.

🛡 TOWER GUARD
3

When a teammate's Princess Tower is destroyed, that player may immediately play one unit card ignoring elixir cost as a last-stand response. This Tower Guard triggers once per tower, once per game.

🏆 2v2 Winning & Key Rules

Winning

First team to destroy the enemy King Tower wins. If decks run out: most total wounds on all enemy towers wins.

Shared Tower Rules

  • Either teammate can play spells affecting their shared towers on their own turn
  • When first Princess Tower falls, both players benefit from the King Tower awakening to 2w/turn
  • Tower Guard (free unit on tower death) can only be used by the player whose lane that Princess Tower is in

Deck Building for 2v2

  • Each player still follows all standard deck requirements
  • Coordinate before the game: a Ground-heavy deck in Lane A pairs well with Air/Spell in Lane B
  • Elixir Pass makes cheap flexible cards more valuable — consider keeping a 3⚡ card ready to send

Quick Reference

All rules at a glance — print this tab and keep it beside the board

Turn Order

1
Deploy — spend elixir, play cards to your Back row or Neutral zone
2
Advance — non-Building units move Back→Front→Neutral each turn automatically
3
Combat — roll D20 per unit. Neutral zone attacks first, then Front, then Back
4
Tower fire — Princess Tower fires 1w in its own lane (Neutral+Front only, not Back). King fires both lanes when active.
5
Opponent's turn

D20 Combat Table

1 — MISS
0 wounds. Towers still deal 1w flat.
2–8 — WEAK
1 wound
9–16 — HIT
2 wounds
17–19 — CRIT
3 wounds
20 — ⚡ CS!
3 wounds + trigger Clash Strike effect

Attack Distance

⚔ Ground/Air
Back: no attack · Front: hits Neutral only · Neutral: hits all + tower if clear
🏹 Ranged
Back: hits Neutral · Front: hits Neutral + can hit tower · Neutral: hits all + tower
Building
Fires 1 zone ahead. Never attacks towers. Never moves.

Towers

Princess (8w)
Lane-specific. 1w/turn to nearest enemy in its lane, Neutral+Front only. Falls at 8w.
♛ King (12w)
Active when either Princess falls. 2w/turn, both lanes, Neutral+Front only. Destroy King to win.

Speed & Elixir

⚡ Fast
Deploys to Front row (skips Back)
🧍 Normal / 🐢 Slow
Deploys to Back. 2 turns to reach Neutral.
⚡ Elixir
Start: flip 2 face-up (2⚡). Each turn: rotate spent back, flip 1 new. Max 10⚡.

Card Effects

🟢 ON DEPLOY
Green box. Triggers the moment the card is placed, before combat.
☠ ON DEATH
Orange box. Triggers when unit is eliminated. Cannot be prevented.
⚡ CLASH STRIKE
Purple box. D20=20: 3w + special effect on card.
Skeleton token
Spare D6 laid flat = 2-wound Fast Ground unit.
Frozen
Unit skips combat for 1 turn. Applied by Vine Trap CS or Freeze spell.

Deck Rules

12 unit/spell cards
Max 2 copies of any card
10 elixir cards
Any mix of 4 types
Requirements
1 Tank (5+w Ground) · 1 Air · 1 Ranged · 1 Building · 1 Spell · 1 per set

🎲 Clash Dice — Product Line

Official dice with custom engraved faces matching game terminology. No counting pips — every face shows a game symbol. Available in four set colorways.

⬡
CLASH D20
20-SIDED · COMBAT DIE
The main combat die. Weighted for satisfying rolls. The 20-face has an engraved lightning bolt — unmissable when you hit it.
ENGRAVED FACES
1💨MISS — swoosh glyph
2-8🗡️WEAK — single sword
9-16⚔️HIT — crossed swords
17-19💥CRIT — explosion glyph
20⚡CLASH STRIKE — lightning
⬡
WOUND D6
6-SIDED · UNIT TRACKER
One sits beside each unit card on the board. Rotate up as wounds are added. Custom faces use clash symbols instead of pips — no counting needed.
ENGRAVED FACES
1🗡️1 Wound — scratch mark
2⚔️2 Wounds — crossed blades
3🩸3 Wounds — blood drop
4💀4 Wounds — skull (medium dies)
5💀💀5 Wounds — double skull
6☠️6 Wounds — PEKKA/tank dies
PRINCESS D8
8-SIDED · TOWER TRACKER
Octagonal shape echoes tower cross-sections. 4 per box — one per Princess Tower. Custom tower art degrades across faces as wounds increase.
ENGRAVED FACES
0🏰Pristine — full tower
2Damaged — crack marks
4🔥Half HP — flames appear
6💥Critical — crumbling
8💀FALLEN — skull + rubble
KING D12
12-SIDED · PRESTIGE DIE
Crown-shaped 12-sided die. Heavier than standard, gold-ink engraving. The physical reminder of what's at stake — when it hits 12, the game ends.
ENGRAVED FACES
0Invulnerable — gold crown
4🏰Awakened — starts firing 2w/turn
8🔥Desperate — flames etched
12💀FALLEN — GAME OVER

Starter Pack: 8× D6 · 4× D8 · 2× D12 · 1× D20

Available in Storm Brigade purple, Iron Vanguard red, Jungle Rush green, Shadow Crypt matte black, and Clash Champion gold (tournament edition).

⚔ BATTLE LOG
► ⚖ Balance Reference (v38+)
NERFED (cost too high for wounds):
Knight 4w→3w · Royal Ghost 4w→3w · Hog Rider 5w→4w
Jungle Knight 5w→4w · Royal Recruits +passive

BUFFED (wounds too low for cost):
Three Musketeers 4w→5w · Ranger 3w→4w
Rascals 2w→3w+passive · Royal Hogs +deploy passive

PASSIVES ADDED:
PEKKA ON DEPLOY · Night PEKKA ON DEPLOY
Sparky ON DEATH · Shaman ON DEATH · Royal Hogs ON DEPLOY
👥 2v2 BATTLE

Game Simulation

Storm Brigade vs Shadow Crypt · 8-round scripted game · real D20 rolls

A full game plays out step by step on a real board. Tap Step to advance one action, Auto Play to watch it run, or Rewind to go back. D20 rolls animate on-screen for every attack.

START
⚔ BATTLE LOG 0 events
Press Step or Auto Play to begin

Game Board

Top-down table view · Click cards to wound · Click tower dots to track HP

A top-down view of your physical play mat. Click a unit card to advance its D6 wound die. Click tower HP dots to track damage. Both Princess Towers must fall before the King can be attacked.

⚔ HOW TO PLAY
STEP-BY-STEP TUTORIAL
Step 1 of 12
STEP 01 · WELCOME
Welcome to Clash Royale TCG!
⚔️🃏
💡
This is a physical card game played with real cards. This app is your digital scoreboard — tracking HP, wounds, elixir, and combat rolls so you can focus on strategy.
🎯
Goal: Destroy your opponent's King Tower. To reach the King, you must first destroy at least one of their Princess Towers.
STEP 02 · THE BOARD
The 5-Zone Battle Board
🔴 CPU BACK ROW
🔴 CPU FRONT ROW
⚔️ NEUTRAL ZONE
🟣 YOUR FRONT ROW
🟣 YOUR BACK ROW
📋
The board has 2 lanes (A & B) and 5 zones. Each zone holds max 3 cards. Units advance forward each turn from Back → Front → Neutral.
💡
Deploy cards to your Back Row first. They automatically advance toward the enemy each turn.
STEP 03 · ELIXIR
Elixir — Your Resource
⚡⚡⚡⚡⚡⚡⚡⚡⚡⚡
⚡
You start with 2 elixir and gain 1 per turn up to a max of 10. Each card costs elixir to deploy — shown as a purple number on the card.
💡
Elixir management is key! A 5-elixir card is powerful but expensive. Sometimes 2 cheap cards are better than 1 expensive one.
EXAMPLE
You have 6 elixir. You play a Giant (5⚡) — leaving 1 elixir. Next turn you'll have 2⚡ to spend.
STEP 04 · YOUR CARDS
Your Hand & Deck
🃏🃏🃏🃏
🃏
You see 4 cards in hand at all times. When you play one, a new card is drawn from your deck. Your deck has 10 unique cards that cycle.
💡
Tap a card to select it (it highlights). Then tap a zone on the board to deploy it there. Tap the card again to deselect.
👆
Long-press any card to see its full stats and ability text before playing it.
STEP 05 · DEPLOYING
How to Deploy Cards
🟣 BACK ROW
▼ tap empty slot ▼
📌
Ground units deploy to your Back Row. Fast units (blue badge) auto-deploy to Front Row if space is available. Buildings stay in place and never advance.
💡
You can deploy cards to Lane A or Lane B. Split your forces between lanes to pressure both sides, or stack one lane to overwhelm the enemy.
TRY IT
Go to vs CPU, tap a card in your hand, then tap a ▼ Deploy slot in your back row to place it.
STEP 06 · ADVANCING
Units Move Forward
🟣 BACK → 🟣 FRONT → ⚔️ NEUTRAL
➡️
At the End of Turn, all units advance one zone forward: Back → Front → Neutral. Units in Neutral attack enemy units or towers each combat round.
💡
Deploy cards 2 turns before you want them fighting. A Back Row card takes 2 turns to reach Neutral.
STEP 07 · COMBAT
Rolling D20 to Attack
🎲 ROLL D20
🎲
When units clash in the Neutral zone, tap Roll D20! for each attacker.
1 = Miss · 2–8 = 1 wound · 9–16 = 2 wounds · 17–19 = 3 wounds · 20 = ⚡ Clash Strike!
⚡
A Clash Strike (20) deals 3 wounds AND triggers your card's special ability! These can turn the tide of battle.
STEP 08 · WOUNDS
HP & Wounds to Kill
❤️❤️❤️❤️ → 💀
❤️
Each card has a Wounds to Kill (WTK) number. When a card takes that many wounds, it is eliminated from the board. The HP bar on each token shows current health.
EXAMPLE
A Knight (WTK 4) takes 2 wounds from a D20 roll, then 1 more from a tower. That's 3 total — one more wound eliminates it.
STEP 09 · TOWERS
Princess & King Towers
🏰 8HP · 12HP
🏰
Princess Towers (8 HP each) fire 1 wound/turn at the nearest enemy in Neutral or Front zone. King Tower (12 HP) activates once a Princess Tower falls — firing 2 wounds/turn.
💡
Towers fire automatically at the end of each round. Use tanky units like Giant to absorb tower fire while your damage dealers push through.
STEP 10 · SPELLS
Spell Cards
⚡ ZAP · 🔥 FIREBALL · 🌪️ TORNADO
✨
Spells have instant effects — they don't deploy to the board. Play a spell from your hand and choose a target zone or unit. Spells ignore the Back Row deployment rule.
💡
Use Zap to stun a threatening unit for 1 turn. Use Fireball to hit a group. Use Tornado to push enemies back and buy time.
STEP 11 · STRATEGY
Key Strategy Tips
🧠 THINK AHEAD
⚡
Elixir advantage: Try to get more value than you spend. If they play a 5-cost card, counter it with a 3-cost card.
🏰
Protect your towers: Don't let enemy units reach your towers uncontested. Always have defenders in your lanes.
⚔️
Split push: Deploying in both Lane A and B forces your opponent to defend everywhere at once.
🃏
Know your deck: Build your deck in the Deck Builder before playing. A balanced deck has tanks, damage dealers, and at least 1 spell.
STEP 12 · LET'S PLAY!
You're Ready to Battle!
⚔️
🏆
You know the basics! Deploy smart, roll dice, protect your towers, and destroy theirs. Good luck!
📖
For deeper rules and card abilities, check the Rules and Reference tabs in the nav. The Deck Builder lets you customize your cards before battle.
